news.title: ‘Burnham bounce’ and World Cup lift mood in July, says data firm
news.lede: ‘Wave of optimism’ is sparked by prospect of new PM, England football team’s progress and hot weather. Consumer confidence in the UK jumped this month by its biggest margin for nearly three years as the prospect of Andy Burnham becoming prime minister became almost certain. The data company GfK said a “Burnham bounce” along with a strong run by the men’s England football team at the World Cup and sunny weather had lifted the mood of consumers in its long-running monthly survey. Burnham’s byelection victory on 18 June, which put the former mayor of Greater Manchester in pole position to take over from Keir Starmer, was credited with helping to improve consumers’ outlook for the economy over the next year by eight points.

news.title: Bouaddi, Moussa and the Arab World Cup stars set for moves in club football
news.lede: Following a historic eight-team Arab participation in the World Cup, several players from the Middle East and North Africa are hoping to make big moves during this summer’s transfer window. Morocco’s Ismail Saibari, who scored three goals in three games for the Atlas Lions at the World Cup, completed a switch from PSV Eindhoven to Bayern Munich last month, during the competition, while Egyptian star Mohamed Salah has everyone waiting on the announcement of his next destination following his Liverpool exit, with Besiktas emerging as the leading contender. Here’s a look at other Arab standouts who have garnered attention for their World Cup performances and are expected to land new deals in the coming weeks.

news.title: The Remains of 39 Days
news.lede: As it ended, the 23rd edition of the FIFA World Cup revived an old argument: Can the sport ever be walled off from politics? Trouble shadowed the tournament from its early days—a US president intervening to have a player’s ban lifted, the FIFA chief admitting he had fretted over Argentina’s possible exit, and a banner unfurled after a semi-final reasserting Argentina’s claim over the Falklands (Las Malvinas). All of this added to suspicions that refereeing and stagecraft alike tilted towards expedience. Not for nothing Argentina, playing presumably Lionel Messi's last World Cup, drew the fiercest scrutiny: rival fans and commentators pointed to contentious VAR decisions in matches against Egypt and England as evidence of favouritism, even as the US investigates the Argentine Football Association’s (AFA) finances.

news.title: 'My last match was World Cup final': Argentina defender Nicolas Otamendi announces international retireme
news.lede: Argentina defender Nicolas Otamendi has announced his retirement from international football, ending a remarkable 17-year career with the national team. The 38-year-old leaves as one of Argentina's most successful defenders, having won the 2022 FIFA World Cup and two Copa America titles. His final appearance came in Argentina's 1-0 defeat to Spain in the FIFA World Cup final in the United States.Otamendi made 139 appearances for Argentina and was a key figure in the team's success over the years. After the final, he shared an emotional message on Instagram, thanking the country for giving him the chance to achieve his biggest dream."

news.title: 'He beats us even in socks': Amateur players can't believe as World Cup star Michael Olise joins random N
news.lede: Just days after helping France finish fourth at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, Bayern Munich winger Michael Olise traded football's biggest stage for a casual kickabout in New York City, leaving a group of amateur players stunned after unexpectedly joining their match. Football News: After a dramatic World Cup, Michael Olise of Bayern Munich astounds amateur players in NYC by joining their casual match, showcasing talent in socks. Discover the surprise encounter and his future in football.

news.title: Lionel Messi Delays His Argentina Retirement and Will Receive a Historic Farewell
news.lede: Lionel Messi's final chapter with Argentina will have to wait a little longer. Following the painful defeat to Spain in the 2026 FIFA World Cup final, speculation quickly grew that the Albiceleste captain could announce his international retirement. However, everything indicates that the moment has not yet arrived.

news.title: Norway considers filing Fifa ethics complaint over Trump role in World Cup red card saga
news.lede: Norwegian federation president: Fifa on a ‘slippery slope’. Trump called Infantino for review of Balogun’s red. Donald Trump’s role in clearing Folarin Balogun from a mandatory one-game ban at the World Cup is set to provoke a formal complaint to Fifa ethics investigators. The Norway soccer federation said Thursday its board of directors will consider filing the complaint at its next meeting, likely on 6 August.

news.title: The president of the Argentine Football Association breaks his silence: do not be fooled by "conspiracy theories"
news.lede: Tapia admits that Spain deserved the 2026 World Cup title. The 2026 World Cup final won't be forgotten in a hurry. Not just for the joy that swept across Spain after the coronation, but for the wave of controversy and the hundreds of conspiracy theories that erupted in Argentina after Ferran Torres's goal stripped the "tango dancers" of their world crown. Explanations for the defeat have spread like wildfire in Argentina over the past hours.
